{{Quote|I watched them destroy entire cities just to make a point. I watched them load intelligent beings into freighters with almost no life support and ship them off to who knows where… I held my tongue, but I'm not like that.|Nogdra on his reasons for defecting|<ref name="X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>}}
'''Nogdra''' was a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], who later defected to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==Biography==
Nogdra grew up in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], surrounded by people of many different [[Species/Legends|species]]. After joining the Imperial Navy, he served aboard a [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], and witnessed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] commit many atrocities, destroying cities in order to make a point and loading prisoners into freighters with little life support. He found the Empire's actions appalling, and after he was placed in command of the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corellian Corvette]] ''[[Bixby]]'', he and his crew decided to defect.

''Bixby'' was guarding a convoy intended to supply ''[[Operation Strike Fear]]'', when Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] from [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] near the [[Dellalt system/Legends|Dellalt system]] showed up for a hit-and-fade [[Battle in the Dellalt system|skirmish]] against the convoy. ''Bixby'', however, found the opportunity and informed them of their intention to join the Rebellion. The Alliance dispatched the [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|transport]] [[Dagger (Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport)|''Dagger'']] to dock with the corvette and transfer an Alliance team to secure the ship.

Nogdra offered his services as a captain to the Rebels and also informed them that Dellalt system was being used as a staging point for a major Imperial operation, [[Operation Strike Fear]]. This information was later confirmed by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] during a reconnaissance mission in a lone [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]].

==Behind the scenes==
Nogdra seems an anagram of the word 'Dragon'. Although the name is not mentioned inside the game, anagrams of real-world words and names are frequent in the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] game.
